element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundational grid-based tactics established immediately with small force
Learned through mandatory encounters with varied enemy unit types
Strategic positioning choice affecting mobility vs. defense
Narrative-driven stat bonuses rewarding unit pairing and story progression
Required to defeat final boss (Medeus); introduces weapon availability as strategic limitation
Permanent character progression; stat caps and experience cycling through new classes
Multi-stage objective fetch system driving narrative progression
Movement restrictions and supply limitations in new environment
Recontextualizes former ally as primary antagonist; affects player emotional engagement

typical_relief_triggers: Victory conditions met (base captured or all enemies defeated)
Successful unit promotions and stat increases
Acquisition of legendary weapons or new party members
typical_tension_triggers: Introduction of new enemy unit types
Mounted cavalry charges into player formation
Mandatory escort missions protecting NPC units
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based tactical pacing with narrative interludes; tension escalates across Book 1 toward Medeus confrontation, releases at Book 1 completion, immediately re-escalates in Book 2 as political intrigue replaces dragon threat

difficulty_curve.early_game: Deliberately lowered difficulty to encourage new players; small starting force against manageable enemy groups; basic weapon triangle introduces core mechanic safely
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Mounted unit integration increases enemy tactical complexity; legendary weapon quests introduce resource scarcity; support system benefits begin to materialize through mandatory character pairings
difficulty_curve.late_game: Book 1 culminates in high-difficulty Medeus confrontation requiring Falchion acquisition and army cohesion; Book 2 shifts difficulty through multi-stage sphere quests and environmental challenges rather than raw enemy power
notable_spikes: Book 1 finale: Gharnef and Medeus back-to-back fights; Medeus requires Falchion or becomes undefeatable
Book 2 Chapter 1: Narrative betrayal and immediate hunted status forces tactical retreat
Book 2 sphere collection arc: Desert terrain hazards introduced alongside increased enemy density

notable_level_patterns.description: Book 1 starts with Marth and small core force (Jagen mentor figure, Caeda ally), progressively recruits additional units through story encounters. Each recruitment moment serves as both narrative beat and gameplay expansion.

Progression locked behind acquisition of specific legendary weapons. Falchion required to defeat Medeus; multiple spheres in Book 2 gate final confrontation. Creates extended narrative tension around obtaining specific objects.
Book 1 concludes in relative homeland safety; Book 2 immediately introduces desert of Khadein as hostile alien environment, functionally resetting terrain familiarity and introducing new hazard types
Hardin introduced as ally in Book 1 becomes antagonist in Book 2 without character model or moveset change. Player familiarity with his tactics becomes tactical disadvantage.
